message:
fix(storage): graceful fallback for all blocked cache read operations

Overhaul every public method that reads from blocked_issues_cache
(get_ready_issues, get_blocked_ids, is_blocked, get_blockers,
get_blocked_issues) with a two-tier fallback strategy:

1. If ensure_blocked_cache_fresh() fails (missing/corrupted cache
   table), fall back to recover_blocked_ids / recover_blocked_issues_map
   which recompute the blocker graph directly from the dependencies
   table via compute_blocked_issues_map_impl()
2. If the cache query itself fails after successful refresh, use the
   same fallback path
3. If JSON parsing of blocked_by fails in get_blockers or
   get_blocked_issues, fall back rather than erroring

Structural changes:
- Extract compute_blocked_issues_map_impl() from rebuild_blocked_cache_impl
  (graph computation separated from INSERT/serialization)
- New helpers: recover_blocked_issues_map, recover_blocked_ids,
  blocker_refs_to_issue_ids, load_blocked_issues_from_map,
  query_ready_issue_candidates, query_ready_issues_without_cache
- query_ready_issue_candidates gains exclude_blocked_in_sql and
  apply_limit params for cache-unavailable mode
- Sort/dedup of blockers moved into compute_blocked_issues_map_impl

Tests:
- Replace error-asserting tests with fallback-asserting versions for
  malformed cache JSON
- Add tests for fallback when cache table is entirely missing
  (get_blocked_ids, is_blocked, get_ready_issues)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
